_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval. Result of the FIT-positive diagnostic colonoscopy Mr. DiMarco was referred for.

procedure.name: Colonoscopy
procedure.indication: Positive screening FIT (2026-06-15); asymptomatic — diagnostic colonoscopy on the FIT-positive pathway
procedure.prep_quality: Boston Bowel Prep Scale 8/9 — adequate (right 3, transverse 2, left 3)
procedure.extent_reached: Cecum (confirmed by appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve); terminal ileum intubated

procedure_report: PROCEDURE: Colonoscopy with polypectomy. INDICATION: positive screening FIT (2026-06-15) in an asymptomatic 63-year-old man; FIT-positive diagnostic pathway. PERIPROCEDURAL: OTC aspirin 81 mg daily continued through the procedure (low-dose ASA does not require interruption for colonoscopy/polypectomy). SEDATION: moderate (midazolam/fentanyl), tolerated well. PREP: adequate, BBPS 8/9. FINDINGS: The colonoscope was advanced to the cecum with confirmation of the appendiceal orifice and ileocecal valve; the terminal ileum was intubated and normal. A 9 mm sessile polyp was identified in the ascending colon and removed by hot snare polypectomy; specimen retrieved (Specimen A). A 5 mm sessile polyp in the sigmoid colon was removed by cold snare polypectomy; specimen retrieved (Specimen B). A 3 mm rectal polyp was removed by cold forceps (Specimen C). Scattered non-complicated diverticula were noted in the sigmoid colon without stigmata of bleeding. There was no mass, no ulceration, and no source of active bleeding to explain the positive FIT; the polyps are the presumptive source. Hemostasis adequate at all polypectomy sites; a single prophylactic hemoclip was placed at the ascending colon site given continued aspirin. IMPRESSION: Three polyps removed (9 mm ascending, 5 mm sigmoid, 3 mm rectal); sigmoid diverticulosis. No malignancy or bleeding source identified. Pathology pending.

findings.action_taken: hot snare polypectomy, retrieved; prophylactic hemoclip placed

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort. Post-polypectomy precautions reviewed. Advised on return precautions for GI bleeding (rectal bleeding, black stools, abdominal pain, lightheadedness).

followup.recommendation: Review pathology on all three polyps (ascending 9 mm, sigmoid 5 mm, rectal 3 mm); call patient with results and set surveillance interval based on histology, size, and count
Continue OTC aspirin 81 mg daily as before — it was not held and no dose change is needed; confirm patient understands it is now documented in the record
Ensure the mis-attributed active C. difficile infection is corrected/removed from the problem list (belongs to spouse); no stool testing or isolation indicated

planted_followup_items.description: Histology pending on three polypectomy specimens (9 mm ascending, 5 mm sigmoid, 3 mm rectal). Results must be communicated to the patient and determine the surveillance colonoscopy interval.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with pathology results and document surveillance interval per histology/size/count

planted_followup_items.description: Surveillance colonoscopy interval to be set once pathology returns (multiple polyps including a 9 mm lesion — likely 3-5 year interval if tubular adenomas, sooner if advanced histology).
planted_followup_items.expected_action: assign and document surveillance interval; enter recall

planted_followup_items.description: Aspirin 81 mg was continued through the procedure (not held); confirm patient continues it and that this previously-undocumented antiplatelet is now recorded in the medication list.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: verify aspirin documented and continued; no resumption action needed since not held

planted_followup_items.description: Mis-attributed active Clostridioides difficile infection on the problem list actually belongs to the patient's spouse; patient asymptomatic and never diagnosed.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: remove/reclassify C. difficile infection from patient problem list; no stool testing or isolation

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: Throat's a bit scratchy and I'm pretty gassy and bloated since yesterday. Otherwise okay.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating; no red flags
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; sore throat and gas/bloating are expected after colonoscopy and self-resolve within 24-48h; encourage walking and hydration.
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no bleeding or pain; no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: The doctor took out a few polyps. When will I know what they were, and is it okay that I kept taking my baby aspirin?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about pending polyp pathology and about continued low-dose aspirin
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the result: pathology expected ~2026-08-03, team will call with results and the surveillance interval; confirm the 81 mg aspirin was intentionally continued, is now documented, and no change is needed.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan; maps to pending_pathology (F1) and reinforces the med reconciliation (F3).

contacts.verbatim: I just passed a bunch of dark red blood in the toilet — twice in the last hour — and I'm feeling shaky and a little dizzy when I stand up.
contacts.clinical_signal: Delayed post-polypectomy hemorrhage with orthostatic symptoms; bleeding risk heightened by uninterrupted low-dose aspirin and the 9 mm hot-snare ascending resection
contacts.expected_action: Escalate immediately for same-day ED evaluation; assess hemodynamics/CBC; anticipate repeat colonoscopy for hemostasis at the ascending polypectomy site; review antiplatelet status.
contacts.rationale: Delayed post-polypectomy bleeding with a hemodynamic symptom (lightheadedness) is a genuine red-flag complication requiring urgent evaluation; the continued aspirin (F3) is the relevant bleeding-risk factor.
